summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases. Case ZC2025-007 involved a request to rezone 28.7 acres from M1/B2 to Planned Development Mixed Use (PDM) for a 230-unit apartment and townhome community proposed by PLK Communities; the commission voted unanimously to recommend denial based on inconsistency with the Growth Management Plan and zoning resolution requirements. Case ZC2025-008 involved a request to rezone 16.16 acres from R3 to Planned Development Residential (PDR) for 34 paired ranch-style condominiums by Capital Properties; the commission voted unanimously to recommend approval with stipulations including 5,000 sq ft lot assignments per unit and sidewalks on both sides of the road.
decisions: ZC2025-007: Motion to recommend denial of rezoning from M1/B2 to PDM for 230-unit apartment/townhome development passed unanimously (Currie-Yes, Dinwiddie-Yes, Lewis-Yes, Otteke-Yes, Purcell-Yes)

ZC2025-008: Motion to recommend approval with stipulations of rezoning from R3 to PDR for 34-unit paired ranch condo development passed unanimously (Currie-Yes, Dinwiddie-Yes, Lewis-Yes, Otteke-Yes, Purcell-Yes)
Minutes from September 2, 2025 meeting approved unanimously
action_items: ZC2025-007 to proceed to Board of Trustees for decision on November 18, 2025
ZC2025-008 to proceed to Board of Trustees for decision
Traffic impact study required by Clermont County Engineer for ZC2025-007
Traffic impact study required by Clermont County Engineer for ZC2025-008
ZC2025-008 developer to incorporate 5,000 sq ft lot assignments per unit and sidewalks on both sides of the road per staff stipulations
key_amounts: $45 million estimated project investment for ZC2025-007 (PLK Communities apartment development)
230 apartments and townhomes proposed in ZC2025-007 across 28.7 acres
7.99 units per acre density proposed for ZC2025-007 (max 8 allowed under PDM)
34 residential units (17 paired ranch condos) proposed in ZC2025-008 across 16.16 acres
52% open space proposed for ZC2025-008 (25% minimum required)
6,750 sq ft per lot proposed for ZC2025-008 units (5,000 sq ft staff minimum recommendation)
notable_topics: Rezoning request from M1/B2 to PDM for 230-unit apartment/townhome development (ZC2025-007)
Dispute over whether State Route 28 PDO or Growth Management Plan governs ZC2025-007 property
Split zoning issues on the ZC2025-007 parcel and 2018 rezoning history
Growth Management Plan density limits (1.5 units/acre rural suburban vs 8 units/acre transitional residential)
Clermont County Planning Commission recommended approval of ZC2025-007 but Township staff recommended denial
Rezoning request from R3 to PDR for 34-unit paired ranch condo extension (ZC2025-008)
Recent minimum lot size changes from 10,000 sq ft to 25,000 sq ft necessitating PDR application for ZC2025-008
Resident concerns about traffic congestion on Sandwood Drive and State Route 48 access
Adjacent property owner concerns about buffer zones and existing recreational use of neighboring land
attendees: Mr. Frisbee (Zoning Commission member - absent/no response at roll call)
Mr. Pegram (Zoning Commission member - no response at roll call)
Mick Oakes (PLK Communities - applicant for ZC2025-007)

notes: The transcript contains significant garbled/corrupted sections likely due to audio recording issues. ZC2025-007 had a prior history - it was previously reviewed in 2024 and denied by the Board of Trustees due to a misconception about the entire parcel being zoned M1. The applicant PLK Communities argued the State Route 28 PDO should govern rather than the Growth Management Plan, while Township staff disagreed. Clermont County Planning Commission and Goshen Township staff reached opposite recommendations on ZC2025-007. Trustee Allen was referenced regarding the prior 2024 split decision. Mr. Frisbee and Mr. Pegram appear to have been absent from the meeting based on roll call responses. The next Zoning Commission meeting is scheduled for December 2, 2025.
title: ZC 11-4-2025
